{{Quote|We're in an Imperial dungeon ship… the kind they used to transport Jedi Knights during the Clone Wars… and it looks like we've reached our destination!|Luke Skywalker to R2-D2|<ref name="DE2">[[Dark Empire 2|''Dark Empire'' 2]]</ref>}}
The '''''Lictor''-class dungeon ship''', also known as an '''Imperial dungeon ship''' or '''Mandalorian Dungeon Ship''', was a versatile warship and mobile prison based on a [[Mandalorian dungeon ship|design]] used by the [[Mandalorian Neo-Crusaders/Legends|Mandalorian Neo-Crusaders]] during the [[Mandalorian Wars]]. The design proved resilient enough that it was still in use nearly four thousand years after its conception.

==Characteristics==
[[File:Dungeon_ship_holding_cell.jpg|thumb|left|150px|A [[Force-sensitive holding cell]] aboard a dungeon ship]]
The models used by the [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Galactic Empire]] were equipped with [[Universal Energy Cage|universal energy cages]], holding cells, drop bays, and sparring chambers where [[Dark side adept/Legends|Dark Side Adepts]] could battle captive [[Jedi/Legends|Jedi]]. They were designed to intimidate, and gained a reputation for never returning a captive who entered them. Guard [[Droid/Legends|droids]] were used to help prevent Jedi escapes.

In order to ensure that prisoners couldn't revolt and commandeer a vessel, their [[Navigation computer/Legends|navicomps]] only were able to hold two sets of coordinates, the home base and the destination, and so could only go to those locations.

Though the ''Lictor''-class wasn't manufactured for combat, it did possess 10 quad turbolaser batteries and two tractor beam projectors for self-defense.

In addition to the [[MandalMotors/Legends|MandalMotors]] design, there was also the scaled down [[Kiltirin-class dungeon ship|''Kiltirin''-class]], manufactured by [[Rendili StarDrive/Legends|Rendili StarDrive]].

==History==
The ''Lictor''-class was used to transport normal and [[Force-sensitive/Legends|Force-sensitive]] prisoners, such as captured [[Jedi Knight/Legends|Jedi Knights]] during the height of the [[Great Jedi Purge/Legends|Great Jedi Purge]] following the [[Clone Wars/Legends|Clone Wars]]. The [[Revelator (Lictor-class)|''Revelator'']] was dispatched to accompany [[Inquisitorius/Legends|Inquisitor]] [[Valin Draco]] on a mission to the [[Cularin system/Legends|Cularin system]], not long after the end of the Clone Wars.<ref name="EotJ">{{DoD|Echoes of the Jedi}}</ref>

During the aftermath of the [[Imperial Civil War]] in [[10 ABY/Legends|10 ABY]], [[Jedi Master/Legends|Jedi Master]] [[Luke Skywalker/Legends|Luke Skywalker]] and [[R2-D2/Legends|R2-D2]] were transported on an Imperial dungeon ship to [[Byss/Legends|Byss]], the secret throne world of the resurrected [[Galactic Emperor/Legends|Emperor]] [[Palpatine/Legends|Palpatine]]. By that time, a dungeon ship could be purchased for 7.8 million [[Galactic Credit Standard/Legends|credits]].

==Behind the scenes==
[[File:DungeonShip-DE.png|thumb|right|An Imperial dungeon ship]]
''[[Star Wars: Republic: Dreadnaughts of Rendili]]'' indicates that the dungeon ships were refitted from [[Dreadnaught-class heavy cruiser/Legends|''Dreadnaught''-class heavy cruisers]] captured during the [[Battle of Rendili]]. These were based on a Mandalorian design similar to ''Lictor''-class dungeon ships, and so converted into them.
